---
title: "Working with Workflows"
format: html
---

## Why a 'Pathway Flowchart'?

::::: columns
::: {.column width="40%"}
For many, creating a diagram of your workflow is an intuitive approach to identifying the parts of the process, and from there we can start to think about how to transition those parts into an open source framework. If this approach feels right for you, then this template serves to simplify this adoption in an open source way.

Sure, you can sketch your workflow on a piece of paper, but by following this process, you will learn skills, and the diagrams you develop can be integrated into your developing open source framework. Ultimately, do what is best for you, but know this template is here for you to follow.
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}
![Workflow diagram for creating the Pathway Flowchart project. This project will create interactive diagrams that facilitate a visual representation of your workflow and the process of incremental adoption of an open science framework for each component of your workflow.](images/OSworkflow.jpg){width="385"}
:::
:::::

## What do I need to Start?

You will need to work in R/Quarto (RStudio or Positron) and Github. We also recommend Github Desktop to facilitate working locally and saving to the cloud (github). If you are new, we recommend the [PSAW 2022 R/Github Training Sessions](https://rverse-tutorials.github.io/PSAW-2022/) to get started.

## Then what?

1.  **Getting Started:** Create your own Pathways Repo by saving this template to your own Github Repo

2.  **Flowchart Build:** Build a Flowchart using your existing documentation and/or the draw.io web application

3.  **Flowchart Refine:** Refine your flowchart and prepare for Pathways Flowchart

4.  **Pathways Flowchart:** Compile surveys for your Pathways Flowchart

5.  **Decision Tool:** The decision tool will evaluate your pathways survey responses to identify workflow components for prioritization.

6.  **Open Science Dashboard:** Use keywords to find a template to use or an example to fork. Cheating is Encouraged!

7.  **Get it Done:** Work on your project! This is independent of the Pathways Flowchart, but we encourage you to find a community to help you tackle your goals.

8.  **Giving Back:** If your project can serve as a good example or if it has new ways of solving a problem, we encourage you to share it as an example for others to consider!

9.  **Revise and Revisit:** Revise and Revisit your workflow, and move onto the next project!

Follow the button on the bottom right to get started (or follow the process using the left sidebar)
